summary: Restored the external Dolphin SDK OSMemory.c unit to matching status after the switch to extern/dolphin. The PR added the missing memory-protection implementation, including reset handling, memory interrupt handling, BAT/MMU setup asm helpers for 24MB and 48MB configurations, and __OSInitMemoryProtection. It also renamed the data symbol at .data:0x80402348 from the generic OSMemory_80402348 to ResetFunctionInfo and flipped configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "dolphin/os/OSMemory.c") to Object(Matching, "dolphin/os/OSMemory.c").
what_changed: Added #include "__os.h" to OSMemory.c so the unit can reference internal OS declarations such as __MEMRegs, __OSMaskInterrupts, __OSSetInterruptHandler, OSErrorTable, and related internals.
Added static BOOL OnReset(BOOL final), which masks memory-related interrupts and writes __MEMRegs[8] = 0xFF when final reset is true, then returns TRUE.

Added static void MEMIntrruptHandler(__OSInterrupt interrupt, OSContext* context), which reads memory fault cause/address registers, clears __MEMRegs[0x10], dispatches through OSErrorTable[0xF] when present, and otherwise calls __OSUnhandledException.
Added __MWERKS__-guarded inline asm helpers Config24MB, Config48MB, and RealMode for BAT/MMU reconfiguration and real-mode transition behavior.
Added static OSResetFunctionInfo ResetFunctionInfo initialized with OnReset and priority 0x7F.

Added void __OSInitMemoryProtection(void), including interrupt disable/restore, simulated-memory size checks, calls through RealMode(Config24MB/Config48MB), memory register initialization, five memory interrupt handler registrations, reset-function registration, conditional simulated-memory register setup, and unmasking interrupt bit 0x08000000.
Renamed the symbol at .data:0x80402348 in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from OSMemory_80402348 to ResetFunctionInfo.
Changed configure.py so dolphin/os/OSMemory.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
smart_moves: Restored the unit by adding the missing SDK implementation rather than only changing the build status; the matching flip in configure.py is backed by substantial source recovery.
Used a meaningful data symbol name, ResetFunctionInfo, instead of keeping an address-derived placeholder, making the .data object correspond to the static OSResetFunctionInfo instance in OSMemory.c.
Kept low-level assembly helpers behind #ifdef __MWERKS__, matching the existing pattern needed for CodeWarrior inline asm while avoiding exposing that syntax to other compilers.
Preserved exact low-level register operations in inline asm for BAT setup and real-mode entry, which is appropriate for SDK OS code where instruction selection and ordering are critical to matching.
Included a dummy local stack object, u8 _[40], in __OSInitMemoryProtection, suggesting stack-frame shaping was needed for the matching binary layout.
decomp_lessons: When a file is moved or restored under extern/dolphin, confirm that previously matched SDK units did not lose source-only pieces during the migration; the PR body explicitly says OSMemory 'Was matched before switch to extern/dolphin'.
A NonMatching-to-Matching configure.py change should be paired with source and symbol updates that explain why the unit now matches.
For Dolphin OS internals, adding __os.h may be necessary when public dolphin/os.h is insufficient for internal globals and interrupt/reset helper declarations.
Address-derived symbols in symbols.txt should be renamed once the recovered source identifies the object; here OSMemory_80402348 became ResetFunctionInfo.
Memory-protection and MMU/BAT setup code may require exact inline assembly rather than C approximations, especially around isync, mtdbatu/mtdbatl, mtibatu/mtibatl, mtsrr0/mtsrr1, and rfi sequences.
Dummy local arrays can be a legitimate matching tactic for stack layout, but future work should keep them evidence-grounded and localized.
naming_conventions: Static reset-function objects should use semantic names matching their type and role, e.g. ResetFunctionInfo for an OSResetFunctionInfo object.
Internal Dolphin OS functions retain double-underscore prefixes, e.g. __OSInitMemoryProtection, __OSMaskInterrupts, __OSSetInterruptHandler, and __OSUnhandledException.
The recovered interrupt handler is spelled MEMIntrruptHandler in the diff, including the missing 'e' in 'Interrupt'; future searches should preserve this exact spelling unless later PRs rename it.
Config24MB, Config48MB, and RealMode use PascalCase-style names matching low-level SDK helper naming in this file.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implemented Config24MB and Config48MB as static asm functions under __MWERKS__ with nofralloc to avoid compiler-generated prologue/epilogue.
Used explicit lis/addi sequences with @ha/@l relocations to construct BAT register constants.
Cleared and restored DBAT/IBAT entries with ordered mtdbatu/mtdbatl/mtibatu/mtibatl instructions and isync barriers.
Used mfmsr plus bit manipulation, then mtspr/mtsrr instructions and rfi to enter the desired execution state.
RealMode clears low address bits with clrlwi, writes the target function to SRR0, adjusts MSR bits with rlwinm, writes SRR1, and returns from interrupt with rfi.
Added u8 _[40] in __OSInitMemoryProtection, likely to force stack layout for matching; the diff does not include comments explaining the exact matching constraint.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text evidence says: 'Was matched before switch to extern/dolphin'.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching MEMIntrruptHandler spelling or OSMemory.c formatting to see whether the typo-like name was preserved for matching or renamed.
Search for other extern/dolphin migration regressions where a file was previously matched but became NonMatching after relocation.
Compare other Dolphin OS reset-function objects to confirm consistent OSResetFunctionInfo symbol naming and priority conventions.
Search for uses of dummy local arrays such as u8 _[40] in matched SDK code to understand stack-shaping patterns.
